preSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode тАФ no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ тАФ Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ тАФ Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ тАФ Business logic layer
- src/events/ тАФ Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ тАФ Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ тАФ Configuration files
- tests/ тАФ Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

## Coordination
- Read COORDINATION.md before starting any work
- Update COORDINATION.md after completing your work with:
  - What you did
  - Key decisions and their rationale
  - Warnings or context for the next agent
  - Status of your assigned tasks

# Coordination Log

Use this file to share decisions, status updates, and context with other agents.
Write your updates below. There is no enforced format тАФ use whatever structure helps.

postSessionState.COORDINATION.md:
# Coordination Log

Use this file to share decisions, status updates, and context with other agents.
Write your updates below. There is no enforced format тАФ use whatever structure helps.

---

## Analytics Dashboard API тАФ Completed

**What was done:**
- Created `src/models/analytics.ts` with three interfaces: `AnalyticsSummary`, `UserAnalytics`, `TrendPoint`
- Created `src/services/analytics.service.ts` with `AnalyticsService` class (DI via constructor: `UserRepository`, `OrderRepository`)
  - `getSummary()` тАФ aggregates total users, orders, revenue, and active users (users with at least one order)
  - `getUserAnalytics(userId)` тАФ per-user stats (order count, total spent, last active date)
  - `getTrends(metric, startDate, endDate)` тАФ time-series data grouped by day for 'orders' or 'revenue' metrics
- Created `tests/services/analytics.service.test.ts` with 10 tests covering all methods and edge cases

**Key decisions:**
- "Active users" defined as users who have placed at least one order
- `lastActive` for users with no orders falls back to `user.createdAt`
- Trends use day-granularity bucketing with ISO date string keys
- Only 'orders' and 'revenue' metrics supported; unsupported metrics throw an error
- Followed existing patterns: constructor DI, Logger, repository methods like `findAll` with predicates
